name: tibanna-ff

metadata.name: tibanna-ff
metadata.summary: Tibanna runs portable pipelines (in CWL/WDL) on the AWS Cloud.

documentation:
# Tibanna_ff

This is an extension of Tibanna that integrates with the 4DN/CGAP/SMaHT data portals.

The documentation can be found [here](https://tibanna-ff.readthedocs.io/en/latest).

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.stepfunction.StepFunctionFFAbstract.lambda_type
documentation:
This should be 'pony' or 'zebra' for the real class.
'ffcommon' is not actually used. Just a placeholder.

functions.name: tibanna_ffcommon.exceptions.exception_coordinator
documentation:
friendly wrapper for your lambda functions, based on input_json / event comming in...
1. Logs basic input for all functions
2. if 'skip' key == 'lambda_name', skip the function
3. catch exceptions raised by labmda, and if not in  list of ignored exceptions, added
   the exception to output json
4. 'metadata' only parameter, if set to true, just create metadata instead of run workflow

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.wfr.InputFileForWFRMeta.__init__
documentation:
dimension is '0' for a singleton argument,
'0', '1', '2' ... for a 1d-list argument,
'0-0', '0-1', ... , '1-0', '1-1', ... for a 2d-list argument, and so on.
ordinal is 1 for a singleton,
1, 2, 3, 4, ... for an any-dimensional list argument.
value (uuid for the input file) is never a list

This class defines the structure of an input_file as part of
the workflow_run metadata. It is a single element of an input file list
defined in the InputFilesForWFRMeta class.

documentation: returns a dictionary in the unicorn args input file format

returns a dictionary in the unicorn args secondary input file format

documentation: inpfws is an existing InputFilesForWFRMeta object to add to.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.bucket_name
documentation:
The actual object key name(s) on the S3 bucket - most of the time
this is the same as upload_key(s) but in special cases,
this could include prefixes (e.g in case of 4DN Open Data)

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.extra_file_renames
documentation:
The string or string list to be in the rename field of the secondary_input_files,
which corresponds to the extra files of the uuid(s) of the input_files.
None values, '' or [] are removed from the return list.
If the return list has a single element, it returns the element as a string.
In a situation like [[[],[]],[],[]], None is returned instead.
If rename is not set for the main file, None is returned.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.extra_file_s3_keys
documentation:
The actual object key name(s) on the S3 bucket - most of the time
this is the same as upload_key(s) but in special cases,
this could include prefixes (e.g in case of 4DN Open Data)
None values or '' values are removed from the return list.
If the return list has a single element, it returns the element as a string.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.s3_key
documentation:
The actual object key name(s) on the S3 bucket - most of the time
this is the same as upload_key(s) but in special cases,
this could include prefixes (e.g in case of 4DN Open Data)

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.upload_key
documentation:
The upload_key(s) which may be different from the actual s3 key name(s)
on the S3 bucket (e.g. in case of 4DN Open Data)

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.object_key
documentation:
The object_key is <accession>.<extension> without <uuid>.
Note that this is distinct from upload_key or s3_key.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_extra_file_renames_from_uuid
documentation:
The actual object key name(s) of the extra files on the S3 bucket -
most of the time this is the same as upload_key(s) but in special cases,
this could include prefixes (e.g in case of 4DN Open Data).
rename is a single string from the 'rename' field of an input_file
that matches the uuid.
These methods get_xx_from_uuid operate a single uuid input (not a list)
e.g. if self.uuid is a list, these functions could operate on an element in it.
if self.uuid is a 3d list, these function would still operate on an element in
one of the innermost lists.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_extra_file_s3_keys_from_uuid
documentation:
The actual object key name(s) of the extra files on the S3 bucket -
most of the time this is the same as upload_key(s) but in special cases,
this could include prefixes (e.g in case of 4DN Open Data)

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_extra_file_formats_from_uuid
documentation: returns parsed (cleaned) version of file format (e.g. 'bai') for extra files

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_extra_files_from_uuid
documentation:
returns a list of extra file dictionaries received from the server.
Extra files not in the ready status (e.g. uploaded) are not included in the result.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_file_format_from_uuid
documentation: returns parsed (cleaned) version of file format (e.g. 'bam')

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_s3_key_from_uuid

The actual object key name(s) on the S3 bucket - most of the time
this is the same as upload_key(s) but in special cases,
this could include prefixes (e.g in case of 4DN Open Data)

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_bucket_name_from_uuid

The actual object key name(s) on the S3 bucket - most of the time

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.fe_map
documentation:
returns a format extension map (a mapping between file format and
file extension) retrieved from the portal. The first time it is called,
the result is saved in a cache, to avoid sending redundant requests.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.get_metadata
documentation:
returns the metadata retrieved from the portal for a given id (e.g.
uuid or accession). The first time it is called, the result is saved in
a cache, to avoid sending redundant requests.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.input_files.FFInputFile.parse_s3_url
documentation:
parsing "https://bucketname.s3.amazonaws.com/dir/dir2/file"
into ("bucketname", "dir/dir2/file")
Works for both http and http2 but only works for s3 url which is
https anyway.

Class representation for each element in the input_files field in The
pony/zebra input json (job description).

functions.name: tibanna_ffcommon.generic_qc_utils.get_qc_ruleset_model
documentation:
Validate the user supplied QC rule set again the schema and return a Pydantic model.

Args:
    qc_ruleset (dict): QC rule set

Raises:
    GenericQcException: The supplied QC ruleset has the wrong format

Returns:
    QC_ruleset: Pydantic model

functions.name: tibanna_ffcommon.generic_qc_utils.evaluate_qc_threshold
documentation:
Checks if the given metric (defined in the qc_threshold model, value extracted from qc_json)
satisfies the QC targets.

Args:
    qc_threshold (QC_threshold): Pydantic model
    qc_json (QC_json): Pydantic model

Raises:
    GenericQcException: QC metric {metric} in ruleset not found in QC json
    GenericQcException: The ruleset contains an unsupported operator

Returns:
    str: pass, warn or fail

functions.name: tibanna_ffcommon.generic_qc_utils.evaluate_qc_ruleset
documentation:
For given input file, calculated qc metrics and user defined ruleset,
 returns an updated qc_json with indivifual QC flags set and the overall QC flag

Args:
    input_wf_arg_name (string): input workflow argument that the QCs are validated for
    qc_json_ (Dict): qc json that has been persisted as file
    qc_ruleset_ (QC_rulesets): ruleset that is defined in the workflow

Raises:
    GenericQcException: The overall_quality_status_rule contains metric IDs that are not defined in the rulset.

functions.name: tibanna_ffcommon.generic_qc_utils.check_qc_workflow_args
documentation:
This function performs basic sanity checks on the QC and input files

Args:
    input_file_args (list): List of workflow arguments (input files)
    generic_qc_args (list): List of workflow arguments (Generic QC files)

Raises:
    GenericQcException: workflow argument does not have argument_to_be_attached_to specified.
    GenericQcException: workflow argument's argument_to_be_attached_to does not exist.
    GenericQcException: Exactly one of qc_json or qc_zipped must be true.
    GenericQcException: There is no QC JSON associated with input workflow argument
    GenericQcException: There are 2 Generic QC files associated with input workflow argument. One must have `qc_json` and the other `qc_zipped` set to true.
    GenericQcException: There are more than 2 Generic QC files for input workflow argument

functions.name: tibanna_ffcommon.generic_qc_utils.filter_workflow_args_by_property
documentation:
Takes a list of dictionaries and returns a filtered list of those dicts where
dict[property]==property_value

Args:
    workflow_args (list): List of workflow arguments
    property (str): property to filter for
    property_value (any): property value to filter for

Returns:
    list: Filtered list of dictionaries

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.qc.QCDataParser.parse_qc_json

data_list is a list of individual file content (strings).

Allow entering fields that are not defined in the schema.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.qc.QCDataParser.match_field_type
documentation:
Add entry to qc_json if it's in the schema.
for number type field, remove ',' from the value.
(e.g. 20,000,000 -> 20000000)
for string type field, convert numbers to string.
(e.g. 20 -> "20")
if strict is True, do not include entries that do not match
a field in the schema.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.qc.QCDataParser.number
documentation:
Convert a string into a float or integer
Returns original string if it can't convert it.

This class implements a parser for QC data table files and QC json files
in a way that depends on qc schema (dictionary describing fields)

Class for WorkflowRun that matches the SMaHT Metadata schema
Workflow (uuid of the workflow to run) has to be given.

functions.name: tibanna_smaht.__main__.rerun_many
documentation:
rerun all the jobs that failed after a given time point
filtered by the time when the run failed (stopdate, stophour (24-hour format), stopminute)
By default, stophour should be the same as your system time zone. This can be changed by setting a different offset.
If offset=5, for instance, that means your stoptime=12 would correspond to your system time=17.
Sleeptime is sleep time in seconds between rerun submissions.
By default, it reruns only 'FAILED' runs, but this can be changed by resetting status.

Examples

rerun_many('tibanna_tiger-dev')
rerun_many('tibanna_tiger', stopdate= '14Feb2018', stophour=14, stopminute=20)

functions.name: tibanna_smaht.__main__.stat
documentation:

status can be one of 'RUNNING'|'SUCCEEDED'|'FAILED'|'TIMED_OUT'|'ABORTED'

functions.name: tibanna_cgap.__main__.rerun_many
documentation:
rerun all the jobs that failed after a given time point
filtered by the time when the run failed (stopdate, stophour (24-hour format), stopminute)
By default, stophour should be the same as your system time zone. This can be changed by setting a different offset.
If offset=5, for instance, that means your stoptime=12 would correspond to your system time=17.
Sleeptime is sleep time in seconds between rerun submissions.
By default, it reruns only 'FAILED' runs, but this can be changed by resetting status.

Examples

rerun_many('tibanna_zebra-dev')
rerun_many('tibanna_zebra', stopdate= '14Feb2018', stophour=14, stopminute=20)

functions.name: tibanna_cgap.__main__.stat
documentation:
print out executions with details
status can be one of 'RUNNING'|'SUCCEEDED'|'FAILED'|'TIMED_OUT'|'ABORTED'

classes.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.QualityMetricGenericQcValueModel
documentation: This defines the Tibanna internal QualityMetricGeneric schema. These will be used to
create the portal specific QualityMetricGeneric items via the QualityMetricsGenericMetadata class

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.TibannaSettings.get_reporter
documentation:
a reporter is a generic name for somethign that reports on the results of each step
of the workflow.  For our immediate purposes this will return ffmetadata object
(which is a connection to our metadata repository, eventually it should, through
polymorphism support any type of reporter a user might develop.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.TibannaSettings.get_runner
documentation:
a runner is an object that implements a set api to run the workflow one step at a time.
Currently this is sbg for us.

documentation: get file features

documentation:
returns extra_files field of workflowrun given argname of pp_uuid
the return value is a list of dictionaries.

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.file_key
documentation:
returns file object_key on the bucket, either through argname or
processed file uuid (pf_uuid). To get the file key of a secondary file,
use secondary_format - this option is valid only with pf_uuid.
Getting a file key through argname is mainly for getting non-processed files
like md5 or qc reports

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.status
documentation: check file existence as status either per argname or per pf object

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.update_all_pfs
documentation: update functions for PF

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.add_updates_to_pf
documentation: update md5sum, file_size, status for pf itself

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.add_updates_to_pf_extra
documentation: update md5sum, file_size, status for extra file

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.update_input_extras
documentation:
go through all input arguments that have an extra file to be attached
and for each create a patch_item and add to patch_items.
This allows multiple extra output files corresponding to different input
arguments, or same input argument with different formats
update functions for all input extras

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.get_portal_specific_item_name
documentation:
This function gets the portal specific item names for a given item name. This is necessary because
the nameing of similar items can vary across portals, e.g. "quality_metric_generic" (CGAP) vs 
"quality_metric" (SMaHT)

Args:
    item (str): Name of the item

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: This method need to be implemented in the portal specific layers of this class

methods.name: tibanna_ffcommon.portal_utils.FourfrontUpdaterAbstract.update_generic_qc
documentation:
This function goes through the input files and checks for associated Generic QC files.
If QC files exist, it will create a QualityMetricGeneric item on the portal and attach it to the
input file metadata.

Raises:
    GenericQcException: Could not get qc_json from S3
    GenericQcException: Could not post quality_metric_generic item
    GenericQcException: Could not patch quality_metrics of file {input_file_accession}.
